raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need a bash command that finds duplicate filenames case-insensitive. Use find, basename, tr, sort, uniq -d. For case-insensitive, convert to lower. Command: find /workspace -type f -printf '%f\n' | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]' | sort | uniq -d. But need list of duplicates with original case? maybe just names. Provide command.
